summary: Improved matching for common fighter FallSpecial code in ftCo_FallSpecial.c. The changes mainly reshaped local declarations, fighter access, and floating-point expression sequencing in ftCo_80096900, ftCo_800969D8, and ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys without indicating behavioral intent changes.
what_changed: Replaced direct gobj->user_data fighter access with GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in the touched FallSpecial functions.
Split combined declaration-and-initialization of ftCo_Fighter* and ftCo_DatAttrs* into separate declarations followed by assignments.
Removed an unused padding local from the SOLUTION == 1 branch of ftCo_80096900, while preserving the padding local in ftCo_800969D8.
In ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys, hoisted float locals f1, lstick_x, and base to the top of the function.
Rewrote aerial drift computations to store ca->air_drift_stick_mul into f1, multiply by lstick_x, then add base via a temporary rather than using a single inline expression.
Changed drift clamping logic from mutating a local mv_x8 variable to using a macro alias for fp->mv.co.fallspecial.x8 and a ternary assignment for the signed clamp value.
smart_moves: Using GET_FIGHTER(gobj) instead of direct gobj->user_data access likely aligned this file with project conventions and produced better matching codegen.
Separating pointer declarations from assignments is a common decomp matching tactic when the compiler's register allocation or instruction ordering differs from compact C.
Breaking the drift formula into explicit load, multiply, and add steps gave finer control over floating-point evaluation order.
Hoisting float locals and using a temporary for f1 + base helped preserve expression reuse and avoid unwanted recomputation in ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys.
The ternary drift clamp expresses the sign-dependent choice compactly while matching the target flow better than the earlier nested if.
decomp_lessons: For fighter common action code, prefer GET_FIGHTER(gobj) when matching expected access patterns rather than assuming direct gobj->user_data is equivalent for codegen.
Matching float-heavy physics code may require explicit temporaries even when algebraically equivalent inline expressions are clearer.
Compiler output can be sensitive to declaration order and whether locals are initialized at declaration time or assigned later.
When clamping signed float values, a ternary can match better than branching that mutates an intermediate local.
Macro aliases such as #define mv_x8 fp->mv.co.fallspecial.x8 may be used as a localized matching aid, but the PR excerpt does not show whether the macro is undefined later.
naming_conventions: Common fighter functions use the ftCo_ prefix, as in ftCo_80096900 and ftCo_800969D8.
Action callbacks retain descriptive suffixes such as ftCo_FallSpecial_IASA and ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys.
Common fighter attribute pointers are named ca and point to fp->co_attrs.
Fighter object pointers are named gobj, and fighter data pointers are named fp.
FallSpecial move variables are accessed through fp->mv.co.fallspecial fields such as x8 and xC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split declarations and assignments for fp and ca to influence load ordering.
Use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) to match the established fighter retrieval pattern.
Hoist float temporaries to function scope to guide register allocation.
Decompose lstick_x * ca->air_drift_stick_mul + base into staged operations using f1 and tmp4.
Compute drift_max separately from the acceleration term passed to ftCommon_8007D140.
Use ABS(drift_max) > mv_x8 followed by a ternary signed clamp to match branch/expression structure.
review_feedback: No review comments were provided in the available slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to the context counts, but its body was not included, so no specific feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftCommon physics functions where inline aerial drift formulas fail to match and require staged float temporaries.
Check whether localized macro aliases for nested move fields are consistently undefined or avoided after matching work.
Search for remaining direct gobj->user_data access in fighter common action files that could be replaced by GET_FIGHTER for matching.
Compare ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys against adjacent common action physics callbacks for recurring drift clamp patterns.
